---
title: "Data_Linkage"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-13"
---

```{r}
# Loading in the 2 datasets
library(tidyverse)
df1 <- read_csv("sampledata1.csv")
df2 <- read_csv("sampledata2.csv")
```

```{r}
library(janitor)     # clean_names()
library(stringr)     # string processing

# Converting all column names to lowercase + snake_case (underscores)
# Example: "FirstName" → "first_name"
df1 <- df1 %>% clean_names()
df2 <- df2 %>% clean_names()

# Rename df2 columns so BOTH datasets use identical names
# This is required for joining later
df2 <- df2 %>%
  rename(
    first_name = first_name,     # already matches after clean_names()
    last_name  = last_name,
    sex        = sex,
    zip_code   = zip,            # zip → zip_code
    birth_date = dob,            # dob → birth_date
    ssn        = ssn,
    phone      = phone_number,    # phonenumber → phone
    email      = email_address    # emailaddress → email
  )
```

```{r}
# Creating a cleaning function
clean_text <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    as.character() %>%          # force everything to text (avoids type issues)
    str_to_lower() %>%          # "John" → "john"
    str_squish() %>%            # remove extra spaces (" john  " → "john")
    str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]", "")  
    # remove ALL non-alphanumeric characters
    # "o'brien" → "obrien"
    # "john-smith" → "johnsmith"
}
```

```{r}
df1_clean <- df1 %>%
  mutate(
    # Standardize first name
    first_name_clean = clean_text(first_name),

    # Standardize last name
    last_name_clean  = clean_text(last_name),

    # Standardize sex (e.g., "M", "Male", " male ")
    sex_clean        = clean_text(sex),

    # Extract ONLY 5-digit ZIP code
    # Handles cases like "60601-1234" → "60601"
    zip_clean = str_extract(as.character(zip_code), "\\d{5}"),

    # Convert date to character so formats match exactly across datasets
    dob_clean = as.character(birth_date)
  )
```

```{r}
df2_clean <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    first_name_clean = clean_text(first_name),
    last_name_clean  = clean_text(last_name),
    sex_clean        = clean_text(sex),
    zip_clean        = str_extract(as.character(zip_code), "\\d{5}"),
    dob_clean        = as.character(birth_date)
  )
```

```{r}
# Check column names are aligned
names(df1_clean)
names(df2_clean)

# Inspect cleaned variables
head(df1_clean %>% 
       select(first_name_clean, last_name_clean, sex_clean, zip_clean, dob_clean))

head(df2_clean %>% 
       select(first_name_clean, last_name_clean, sex_clean, zip_clean, dob_clean))
```

```{r}
# Performing an inner join:
# keeps only records where ALL five cleaned matching variables
# are exactly the same in both datasets
matches <- inner_join(
  df1_clean,
  df2_clean,
  by = c(
    "first_name_clean",
    "last_name_clean",
    "dob_clean",
    "sex_clean",
    "zip_clean"
  ),
  suffix = c("_1", "_2")
)

# Viewing how many matched pairs were found
nrow(matches)

# Looking at the first few matched records
head(matches)
```

```{r}
# crossing() requires unique column names, so adding suffixes to the column names
df1_eval <- df1_clean %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(., "_1"))
df2_eval <- df2_clean %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(., "_2"))

# Creating ALL possible pairs between dataset 1 and dataset 2.
# crossing() creates the Cartesian product:
# every row in df1_clean paired with every row in df2_clean.
all_pairs <- crossing(df1_eval, df2_eval)

# For each pair, we are defining:
# 1. true_match: the UUIDs are actually the same
# 2. predicted_match: my exact-match rule says they match
all_pairs <- all_pairs %>%
  mutate(
    true_match = uuid_1 == uuid_2,

    predicted_match =
      first_name_clean_1 == first_name_clean_2 &
      last_name_clean_1  == last_name_clean_2 &
      dob_clean_1        == dob_clean_2 &
      sex_clean_1        == sex_clean_2 &
      zip_clean_1        == zip_clean_2
  )
```

```{r}
# Counting the four cells of the confusion matrix

tp <- sum(all_pairs$predicted_match & all_pairs$true_match)
# True positive:
# my algorithm said "match" and it really was a match

fp <- sum(all_pairs$predicted_match & !all_pairs$true_match)
# False positive:
# my algorithm said "match" but UUID says it was NOT a true match

fn <- sum(!all_pairs$predicted_match & all_pairs$true_match)
# False negative:
# my algorithm missed a real match

tn <- sum(!all_pairs$predicted_match & !all_pairs$true_match)
# True negative:
# my algorithm correctly said "not a match"

tp
fp
fn
tn
```
```{r}
# Calculating the assignment performance metrics

accuracy <- (tp + tn) / (tp + fp + fn + tn)
# Proportion of all candidate pairs classified correctly

precision <- tp / (tp + fp)
# Among predicted matches, how many were actually correct?

recall <- tp / (tp + fn)
# Among all true matches, how many did your algorithm recover?

accuracy
precision
recall
```

```{r}

```
